subject: Re: Warp tomo reconstruction not matching IMOD
body:
Hey Ricardo,

The possible differences between Warp and IMOD tomogram reconstructions are

1. flipped across the central xy plane due to a different interpretation of tilt-angle
2. shifted due to a different 3D origin (in Warp the 3D origin is implied by the tomogram dimensions, in IMOD it is implied by the reconstruction thickness and the 2D image dimensions)
3. Edge effects - Warp and IMOD handle data from ‘outside the tilt-image’ differently, this can lead to differences in tomograms outside the FOV of the tilt images

I suspect at least one, maybe both of 1,2 being the source of the apparent ‘warping’ you refer to - video attached for easier comparison of images

Cheers,

Alister

￼



> On 20 Mar 2023, at 11:28, Ricardo Righetto <ricardorighetto@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> Hi,
> 
> I'm trying to reconstruct subtomograms in Warp for refinement with M. The tilt series were originally processed, aligned and reconstructed in IMOD, so I imported them into Warp. The data is from a K3 detector.
> 
> In the debugging process we noticed that the tomo reconstructions in Warp don't quite match the IMOD reconstructions.
> In particular, there seems to be a slight difference in magnification or some kind of warping effect (no pun intended), please see PNG snapshots attached. I made sure that the .xf file used in both Warp and IMOD are identical.
> For a sanity check, I've also reconstructed the tomogram using relion_tomo_reconstruct_tomogram and it matches the IMOD reconstruction.
> My suspicion is that I'm doing something wrong in Warp regarding image dimensions or pixel size, but don't know what exactly.
> The headers of both tomogram reconstructions do match regarding dimensions and pixel size, please find them below (first Warp, then IMOD).
> 
> Any idea what is causing this?
> 
> Thank you!
> 
> ***
>
